zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Knight Richard de Hill於1273年出生在英國史洛普爾納什時，他的父親Thomas de Hill19歲，母親Sarah Ann de Parson19歲。他於1293年與Lady Alice Giffard在英國史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1317年在他的家鄉享年44歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭史洛普爾廷頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Knight Richard de Hill于1273年出生在英国史洛普爾納什时，他的父亲Thomas de Hill19岁，母亲Sarah Ann de Parson19岁。他于1293年与Lady Alice Giffard在英国史洛普爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1317年在他的家乡享年44岁，埋葬在英国英格兰史洛普爾廷頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, from Middle English hill, hell, hull ‘hill’ (Old English hyll). Compare Hiller . This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: possibly in some cases from the personal name Hille, a pet form of some name such as Hilger or Hillary .
German: from a short form of Hildebrand or any of a variety of other names, male and female, containing ancient Germanic hild ‘battle’ as the first element.

variants.name: Hillock
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) small hill’, from Middle English hillok, a diminutive of hille (Old English hyll ‘hill’). See Hill .

variants.name: Hills
meanings: English: variant of Hill with genitival -s if a relationship name, attested in Matilda Hilles, 1327 (Somerset), where the genitival form may signify widowhood; or with plural -s if habitational, attested in Johanne de Hyles, 1379 (Bickerstaffe, Lancashire), and Ricardo del Hyles, 1379 (Great Eccleston, Lancashire); or, with post-medieval excrescent -s, probably the most common form.

variants.name: Hiller
meanings: German: metronymic from Hille, a pet form of the female personal name Hildegund (see Hilke ).
German: from a masculine personal beginning with the ancient Germanic element hild ‘strife, battle’.

variants.name: Hil
meanings: Dutch (Van den Hil): topographic name from hil, hul ‘hill’, particularly in the sense of a flight hill above sea level in the southwestern Netherlands.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Knight Richard de Hill was born in 1273, in Nash, Shropshire, England, his father, Thomas de Hill, was 19 and his mother, Sarah Ann de Parson, was 19. He married Lady Alice Giffard in 1293, in Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1317, in his hometown, at the age of 44, and was buried in Whittington, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Knight Richard de Hill est né en 1273, à Nash, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de Hill, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Sarah Ann de Parson, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Lady Alice Giffard en 1293, à Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en 1317, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Whittington, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Knight Richard de Hill 1273 in Nash, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas de Hill 19 und seine Mutter Sarah Ann de Parson 19. Er hat 1293 in Shropshire, England Lady Alice Giffard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb 1317 im Alter von 44 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Whittington, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Knight Richard de Hill è nato il nel 1273 nel Nash, Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas de Hill, aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Sarah Ann de Parson, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Lady Alice Giffard nel 1293, nel Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1317, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolto a Whittington, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Knight Richard de Hillが1273年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアナッシュで生まれた時，父Thomas de Hillは19歳，母Sarah Ann de Parsonは19歳であった。1293年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアでLady Alice Giffardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1317年に故郷で44歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアウィッティントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Knight Richard de Hill(이)가 1273년 영국 슈럽실르 나시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas de Hill(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Sarah Ann de Parson(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1293년 영국 슈럽실르에서 Lady Alice Giffard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1317년 그의 고향에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 휘틴그턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Knight Richard de Hill nasceu em 1273, em Nash, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas de Hill, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Sarah Ann de Parson, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Alice Giffard em 1293, em Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1317, em sua cidade natal, com 44 anos, e foi sepultado em Whittington, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Knight Richard de Hill родился в 1273 г. в местности наш, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas de Hill было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Sarah Ann de Parson, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Alice Giffard в 1293 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в 1317 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности уиттингтон, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Knight Richard de Hill nació en 1273, en Nash, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas de Hill, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Sarah Ann de Parson, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Lady Alice Giffard en 1293, en Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en 1317, en su pueblo, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultado en Whittington, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
